data.title: Applied Industrial Technologies raises dividend by 10.9% to $0.51
data.description:
• Applied Industrial Technologies (AIT) declares $0.51/share quarterly dividend, 10.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.46.
  • Forward yield 0.72%
  • Payable Feb. 27; for shareholders of record Feb. 13; ex-div Feb. 13.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 10.9% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.46 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See AIT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Royce Micro-Cap Trust reports NAV of $11.79 as of Dec. 31, 2025
data.description:
Royce Micro-Cap Trust (NYSE:RMT) reports NAV of $11.79, while the market price at the close of Dec. 31, 2025, was $10.43.

The fund maintained an average weekly trading volume of 429,716 shares.

data.title: Royce Small-Cap Trust reports NAV of $17.69 as of Dec. 31, 2025

Royce Small-Cap Trust (RVT) reports NAV of $17.69, while the market price at the close of Dec. 31, 2025, was $16.10.

The fund continues to demonstrate robust liquidity with an average weekly trading volume of nearly 1.5 million shares.

data.title: Aurora Mobile partners with Shenzhen stock exchange for information delivery
data.description:
• Aurora Mobile (JG) announced a strategic partnership with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange to leverage Aurora's JPush intelligent push notification solution to upgrade SZSE's digital infrastructure.
  • The deal focuses on enhancing SZSE's mobile platform for faster, more reliable delivery of critical market data.
  • Aurora's platform will serve as a vital channel for the timely delivery of regulatory updates, corporate filings, and investor education content across the market.
  • The stock price traded about 4% higher on Tuesday during early hours.

data.title: Intellia rises as FDA lifts clinical hold on Regeneron-partnered gene editing drug
data.description:
Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) added ~15% in the premarket on Tuesday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration lifted a clinical hold on a late-stage trial for its gene editing therapy, nexiguran ziclumeran (nex-z), developed with Regeneron (REGN).

The MAGNITUDE-2 Phase 3 trial was designed to evaluate nex-z in patients with hereditary transthyretin amyloidosis with polyneuropathy (ATTRv-PN).

The FDA imposed a clinical hold on MAGNITUDE-2 and its sister study, MAGNITUDE-2, in October after patients experienced several clinical responses in the liver.

The Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech said that the regulator lifted the hold on MAGNITUDE-2 after reviewing NTLA’s study modifications and mitigation measures, as part of which the company has increased the trial’s target enrollment to about 60 from 50.

MAGNITUDE-2 for patients with transthyretin amyloidosis with cardiomyopathy remains under clinical hold, with the company planning to issue an update once a consensus is reached with the FDA regarding the study’s future.

data.title: CACI to boost U.S. Army readiness with next-gen electronic warfare solutions
data.description:
CACI International (CACI) has been awarded a five-year task order valued at up to $250 million to enhance spectrum dominance for the U.S. Army Capability Program Executive—Intelligence, Electronic Warfare & Sensors (CPE IEW&S).

CACI will leverage its intrinsic knowledge to provide comprehensive integration and sustainment of the technology to advance the Army’s multi-domain EW and spectrum dominance capabilities.

data.title: United Airlines announces major expansion at Chicago O'Hare amid hot demand
data.description:
United Airlines Holdings (UAL) plans to increase its schedule at Chicago O’Hare International Airport to a record level for the summer. The airline is planning 750 daily flights from its home city, which is 200 more than its nearest competitor and the largest schedule ever operated by any airline at the airport. 

The carrier will offer nonstop service to 222 destinations from Chicago, including 47 international cities across Europe, Asia, and South America, and 175 U.S. destinations. United (UAL) will also strengthen regional connectivity beginning in April with new routes from ORD to Champaign/Urbana, Kalamazoo, Lansing, La Crosse, and Bloomington/Normal.

Building on its 2025 expansion, United (UAL) will also launch flights to nine additional U.S. cities, including Santa Barbara, Monterey, Eugene, and Erie, while adding frequencies to 80 existing destinations nationwide, such as Boston, Nashville, Los Angeles, and San Francisco. With these additions, ORD is set to become the third-largest airline hub in the United States. United (UAL) will briefly operate daily service to Guadalajara, Mexico, in June to accommodate travel for major international soccer events, ensuring nonstop access to all hosting cities.

Operationally, United (UAL) highlighted that it led major carriers in on-time arrivals at ORD in 2025 while operating 31% more flights than its nearest competitor. The airline plans to hire approximately 2,500 additional employees in Chicago this year and increase mainline departures by 20% over summer 2025.

United (UAL) noted that it continues to enhance its travel technology suite, with tools such as Connection Saver, which prevented one million missed connections in 2025; app-guided navigation for tight connections; and expanded use of TSA PreCheck Touchless ID and Bag Drop Shortcut services. 

Shares of UAL edged 0.4% higher in premarket trading.

data.title: Zephyr announces increase in private placement
data.description:
• Zephyr Minerals (ZFR:CA) on Tuesday announced that it has increased the size of its previously announced non-brokered private placement to an aggregate of 5,200,000 units at a price of $0.05 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to $260,000.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant of the company. Each warrant will entitle the holder to acquire one additional share at a price of $0.10 per share for a period of two years from the date of issuance.
  • Proceeds received from the private placement will be used for general working capital purposes and to advance permitting on the Dawson Gold project. It is anticipated that certain insiders of the company will participate in the private placement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Camden National Non-GAAP EPS of $1.33 beats by $0.01, revenue of $68.05M beats by $1.7M
data.description:
• Camden National press release (CAC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.33 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $68.05M (+43.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.7M.
  • For the quarter ended December 31, 2025, the Company reported a return on average assets of 1.28%, a return on average equity of 13.01%, and a return on average tangible equity (non-GAAP) of 19.06%.
  • Net interest margin for the fourth quarter of 2025 increased 13 basis points over the third quarter of 2025 to 3.29%, and core net interest margin increased 10 basis points over the same period to 2.92%.
  • GAAP efficiency ratio for the fourth quarter of 2025 was 54.16%, while the non-GAAP efficiency ratio was 51.69%.
  • Book value per share increased 3% from September 30, 2025 to $41.16 as of December 31, 2025, and tangible book value per share (non-GAAP) increased 4% during the same period to $29.69 at year-end.
